steps.title: Find the sentence's main verb
steps.content:
The sentence already has a main verb: consist ("consist of one stressed syllable followed by two unstressed syllables"). The blank appears earlier in the sentence, between the subject Trisyllabic words and the examples "such as 'devious' and 'particle'". If the blank introduces a second finite verb, a finite verb phrase would compete with the sentence's main verb, "consist."
  • Choice A — classified: past participle — acts as a modifier (words classified as dactyls), not a finite verb; does not compete with the main verb ✓
  • Choice B — can be classified: finite verb phrase — competes with the main verb "consist" ✗
  • Choice C — are classified: finite verb phrase — same main-verb conflict ✗
  • Choice D — have been classified: finite verb phrase — same main-verb conflict ✗

steps.title: Confirm the correct choice
steps.content:
Only a non-finite participle can fill the blank without competing with the main verb consist. Classified is the only participle; the other choices are all finite verb phrases that compete with the main verb "consist."

That is the correct answer.
